name: Jarindo and Albertia Loop Trail
name_original: Jarindo y Albertia — circular por la Sierra de Arlaban
description: Hike the Jarindo y Albertia circular route in Sierra de Arlaban, a moderate 6.0-mile trail with diverse scenery and historical sites.
region_intro: Hiking around Legutio offers diverse landscapes, characterized by prominent water bodies and mountainous terrain. The region features the Urrúnaga and Albina Reservoirs, which provide scenic recreational areas. Surrounding these are green meadows and forests, leading up to significant natural viewpoints like Mount Albertia. The area also provides approaches to the southern slopes of Gorbeia Natural Park, expanding the range of hiking possibilities. Best hiking trails around Legutio The most popular hiking route is Jarindo and Albertia Loop Trail, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 1 minute to complete. This moderate route offers diverse scenery and includes historical sites. Another top favourite among local hikers is Albertia Summit (867 m) – Jarindo (896m) loop from Legutio, a difficult 10.6 miles (17.0 km) path. This demanding hike offers panoramic views of the surrounding reservoirs and mountains. Local hikers also love the Jarindo (896m) loop from Legutio, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail leading through forested areas and offering views of the local landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Hiking around Legutio is defined by scenic reservoirs, prominent mountains like Albertia, and lush green meadows and forests. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Legutio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Legutio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Legutio?
A: Legutio offers a diverse range of hiking experiences with &lt;em&gt;over 15 trails&lt;/em&gt;. These include 3 easy routes, 10 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Legutio?
A: Yes, Legutio has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10509815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PresaAlbina – Iturrimari loop from Legutio&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate hike but suitable for all skill levels with easily accessible paths. The recreational areas along the Urrúnaga and Albina reservoirs also offer pleasant, less strenuous walks.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Legutio?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular feature in Legutio. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056834165/jarindo-and-albertia-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarindo and Albertia Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.0-mile circular route offering diverse scenery. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10510592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarindo (896m) loop from Legutio&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.2-mile trail through forested areas.

Q: Are there any trails with significant viewpoints in Legutio?
A: Absolutely. Hiking to the summit of &lt;strong&gt;Mount Albertia&lt;/strong&gt; is highly recommended for magnificent panoramic views of the Urrúnaga, Albina, and Zadorra reservoirs, as well as the Anboto and Gorbeia mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10511243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albertia Summit (867 m) – Jarindo (896m) loop from Legutio&lt;/a&gt; specifically offers these expansive vistas.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes in Legutio?
A: While Legutio is known for its reservoirs and mountains, you can find natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3009759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uguzpe Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; in the broader region. It's a highlight that can be incorporated into longer explorations around Legutio's natural monuments.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Legutio?
A: Legutio's landscape is characterized by prominent water bodies like the Urrúnaga and Albina Reservoirs, surrounded by lush green meadows and forests. You'll encounter mountainous terrain, including significant natural viewpoints like Mount Albertia, and approaches to the southern slopes of Gorbeia Natural Park, offering a mix of serene waterscapes and challenging ascents.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can be explored while hiking in Legutio?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Legutio offers historical intrigue. You can glimpse the old medieval town structure through remnants like the Arch of Christ, and the ancient Romanesque church of San Blas adds cultural interest. Mount Albertia also holds historical significance as a site of Civil War confrontations, with vestiges still present for visitors to explore.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Legutio?
A: The trails in Legutio are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, the stunning views of the reservoirs and mountains, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various difficulty levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Legutio?
A: Legutio's lush green meadows and forests suggest that spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery for hiking. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially around the reservoirs, while winter hiking might be possible on lower-altitude trails, though conditions on higher peaks like Albertia or Gorbeia would require appropriate gear and caution.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Legutio?
A: Yes, experienced hikers seeking a challenge will find suitable routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10511243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albertia Summit (867 m) – Jarindo (896m) loop from Legutio&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.6-mile path requiring very good fitness. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23819915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Oketa – Oketa loop from Zestafe&lt;/a&gt;, a hard hike for those looking for a significant ascent.

Q: Are Legutio's hiking trails dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on every trail can vary, the general character of Legutio with its green meadows, forests, and recreational areas around the reservoirs often makes it a welcoming place for dogs. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in nature reserves or areas with livestock, and to check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there recreational areas along the trails in Legutio?
A: Yes, the shores of the reservoirs, particularly the Urrúnaga, feature beautiful recreational areas. The Zabalain recreational area near Urrúnaga, for instance, is equipped with amenities like tables, barbecues, and playgrounds, making it ideal for breaks or family outings in nature.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This circular trail usually takes around 3 hours and 1 minute to complete. This estimate is for hiking and does not include extended breaks.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for the Jarindo y Albertia circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route in the Sierra de Arlaban. While the exact starting point can vary, it's generally accessible from areas with parking near the base of the Sierra. Specific parking information would depend on your chosen access point, but it's advisable to check the komoot Tour page for detailed trailhead directions.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse terrain, including unpaved paths, designated hiking paths, and some mountain hiking paths, with a small section of road. You'll encounter tracks and easy-to-walk paths, but also sections through pine and lush beech forests. Hikers may also cross streams.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience contrasting scenery. The Jarindo (896m) summit provides impressive, expansive views. In contrast, the Albertia Summit (867 m) is entirely covered by a beautiful beech forest, offering no views from the very top itself. The trail also showcases a variety of trees, including pines, beeches, and oaks.

faqs.question: Are there any historical sites or landmarks on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sierra de Arlaban has historical significance as a setting for the Battle of Villarreal during the Civil War. Along the route, you may encounter memorials and remnants of trenches. The Campa de la Manzanilla Memorial is a specific highlight on the trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed across multiple seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant foliage. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations, requiring appropriate gear.

faqs.question: Is the Jarindo y Albertia trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, specific regulations regarding dogs in the Sierra de Arlaban should be checked. If dogs are permitted, it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to hike in the Sierra de Arlaban?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking this trail within the Sierra de Arlaban. However, it's always a good practice to respect local regulations and private property signs you might encounter.

faqs.question: What should I consider for navigation on this route?
faqs.answer: Due to numerous intersections along the route, carrying a GPS device or using a reliable navigation app like komoot on your phone is highly recommended to stay on track and avoid getting lost.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find sections that overlap with paths such as Artzaintza naturbidea, Euskal Herriko ibilaldia, Senda Tantaidui, and Senda de Albertia, among others.

faqs.question: Is this trail considered family-friendly?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate' difficulty, this trail requires good fitness and sure-footedness. While many paths are easy to walk, the distance and elevation gain might be challenging for very young children or those unaccustomed to hiking. It's best suited for families with older children who have some hiking experience.
